project: Lithos MVP2 Part 3 — New Scouts & Retrieval Integration
description: Adds three new scouts (graph, coactivation/bridge, source-URL) that exploit MVP1's accumulated edge and coactivation data to broaden retrieval reach, wires them into Phase B of run_retrieve, and updates rerank weights to a 10-scout configuration.

userStories.title: StatsStore.get_coactivated method
userStories.description: As the coactivation scout, I want a StatsStore.get_coactivated method so I can look up nodes frequently co-occurring with seed nodes in past retrievals.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsStore gains get_coactivated(node_ids: list[str], *, namespace: str | None = None, limit: int = 20) -> list[tuple[str, int]] that returns (other_node_id, total_count) pairs ranked by count descending
Returns ranked (other_node_id, total_count) pairs; when namespace is provided, filters to coactivation rows matching that namespace
Deduplicates results across seeds
Unit test: seed coactivation data, verify get_coactivated returns correct (node_id, count) pairs in descending order
Unit test: get_coactivated with namespace filter returns only matching rows
make check passes
userStories.notes: Consider testing edge cases like empty node_ids

userStories.title: Graph scout implementation
userStories.description: As the retrieval pipeline, I want a graph scout that finds neighbors of seed nodes in both the wiki-link graph (NetworkX) and the typed edge graph (edges.db), so that retrieval can follow structural relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: New scout_graph(seed_ids, graph, edge_store, knowledge, *, limit, namespace_filter, agent_id, task_id, tags, path_prefix) -> list[Candidate] in scouts.py
Queries NetworkX via graph.get_links(node_id, direction='both') for each seed
Queries edge_store.list_edges(from_id=seed) and edge_store.list_edges(to_id=seed) for each seed
Deduplicates neighbor IDs; scores by edge weight (edges.db) or 0.5 (wiki-links)
Applies namespace_filter and access_scope gating (reuses _passes_namespace_filter, _passes_access_scope)
Applies tags and path_prefix filtering
Returns candidates with scouts=['scout_graph'] and reasons describing the relationship type
Unit test with wiki-link neighbors + typed edges, verifying dedup and filtering
make check passes

userStories.title: Coactivation/bridge scout implementation
userStories.description: As the retrieval pipeline, I want a scout that finds nodes frequently co-occurring with seed nodes in past retrievals, so that empirically useful associations inform current retrieval.
acceptanceCriteria: New scout_coactivation(seed_ids, stats_store, knowledge, *, limit, namespace_filter, agent_id, task_id, tags, path_prefix) -> list[Candidate] in scouts.py
Queries coactivation data for each seed via stats_store.get_coactivated and scores by count (normalized later by merge_and_normalize)
Applies namespace_filter, access_scope, tags, path_prefix gating
Unit test seeding coactivation data and verifying scout returns correct neighbors
make check passes

userStories.title: Source-URL/domain scout implementation
userStories.description: As the retrieval pipeline, I want a scout that finds notes from the same URL family as seed notes, so that research from the same source is connected.
acceptanceCriteria: New scout_source_url(seed_ids, knowledge, *, limit, namespace_filter, agent_id, task_id, tags, path_prefix) -> list[Candidate] in scouts.py
For each seed node with a source_url, normalizes it with normalize_url() from src/lithos/knowledge.py and then extracts the host/domain from the normalized URL
Scans the normalized source URLs held by KnowledgeManager (e.g. iterating _source_url_to_id entries) to find other notes whose host/domain matches the seed notes' domains
Only activated when at least one seed node has source_url set; returns [] otherwise
Applies namespace_filter, access_scope, tags, path_prefix gating
Unit test with seeded source_url notes verifying domain grouping
make check passes

userStories.title: LcmaConfig 10-scout rerank_weights defaults and validator
userStories.description: As the system, I want the rerank_weights config updated to a 10-scout default map with a backwards-compatible validator so that adding new scout keys does not break existing configurations.
acceptanceCriteria: rerank_weights in LcmaConfig is updated with the 10-scout default map: vector: 0.25, lexical: 0.18, exact_alias: 0.10, tags_recency: 0.07, freshness: 0.04, provenance: 0.04, task_context: 0.04, graph: 0.13, coactivation: 0.10, source_url: 0.05 (weights sum to 1.0)
New _fill_and_renormalize_rerank_weights model validator detects missing keys by comparing user-supplied map against _DEFAULT_RERANK_WEIGHTS
If any keys are missing, inserts them with their default values and renormalizes the full map so all values sum to 1.0 (divides each value by current sum)
If all 10 keys are present, skips renormalization but asserts sum ≈ 1.0 within tolerance
Rejects unknown keys with an error, consistent with note_type_priors validation
rerank_weights tests cover the new 10-scout defaults and backward-compatible fill/renormalize behavior
Update test_defaults_allow_mvp1_no_user_config (tests/test_lcma_config.py:123) to assert len(cfg.lcma.rerank_weights) == 10
make check passes
userStories.notes: Implementation should verify legacy 7-key configs are filled and renormalized correctly (sum = 1.0, relative ordering preserved)

userStories.title: Wire new scouts into run_retrieve Phase B
userStories.description: As the retrieval pipeline, I want the three new scouts wired into Phase B of run_retrieve so that their candidates are merged, normalized, and reranked alongside existing scouts.
acceptanceCriteria: run_retrieve imports and calls scout_graph, scout_coactivation, scout_source_url in Phase B (after provenance scout, before Terrace 1 rerank) — consistent with design doc §5.4
Each new scout is called with return_exceptions=True in a gather or try/except — a failing scout does not abort retrieval
scout_graph is seeded from Phase A top results (sequential, seeded from Phase A); all three new scouts run in Phase B
Add scout_graph, scout_coactivation, scout_source_url to ALL_SCOUT_NAMES list
Receipt scouts_fired includes new scout names when they execute
Update test_all_scout_names_has_seven_entries (tests/test_scouts.py:647) to expect count of 10 (adds scout_graph, scout_coactivation, scout_source_url)
Integration test: retrieve with edges.db edges verifies graph scout fires
make check passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002, US-003, US-004 (scout implementations) and US-005 (config)
